new_p_154: The Arizona Land Deal That Lets You Build, Camp, And Live Exactly How You Want
new_p_155: Most land comes with a mile-long list of rules.
new_p_156: HOAs telling you what color your curtains can be. Restrictions on what you can build. Fees for breathing wrong. But not this land.
new_p_157: This is 0.21 acres of pure freedom in Mohave County, Arizona.
new_p_158: No HOA. No busybodies. No one telling you how to live your life. Just you, your land, and endless Arizona skies.
new_p_160: This land is zoned RMH, which means Residential Manufactured Housing.
new_p_161: Translation? You can build your manufactured home right here. It's approved. It's legal. It's ready for you to make it yours.
new_p_162: But here's where it gets really good. You can camp on this land RIGHT NOW. Tent camping or RV camping, up to 14 days at a time.
new_p_163: That's 30 days total per year. Which means you can use your land this weekend while you're planning your build.
new_p_164: Bring your RV. Park it. Spend weekends there. Get a feel for the land. Watch the sunsets. Sleep under the stars. All while you're figuring out your long-term plans.
new_p_166: You've got choices. Hook up to the local electric company if you want traditional power. Or go completely off-grid with solar or wind. Your land. Your rules.
new_p_167: Water works on a haul system, just like most residents in the area. It's your off-grid water solution. Simple. Proven. Reliable.
new_p_169: Direct paved road access. Your regular car gets you there. No 4x4 needed. No mud. No struggling through dirt roads. Just smooth driving right to your property.
new_p_170: You own it with a Warranty Deed. Real ownership. Real security. Legal protection that can't be taken away.
new_p_171: [See Your Land On Google Maps - Click Here]
new_p_173: You're sitting on historic Route 66. That's not just a road. That's a piece of American history.
new_p_174: A living, breathing landmark that people travel from around the world to see. And you get to own a piece of it.
new_p_176: Stunning mountain vistas from your property. The kind of scenery that makes you stop and stare every single morning.
new_p_177: Kingman is close by. Schools for the grandkids. Shops for groceries.
new_p_178: Restaurants for date night. Everything you need without being crowded or noisy.
new_p_179: The Arizona Route 66 Museum brings history to life just minutes away.
new_p_180: Keepers of the Wild Nature Park shows you the natural beauty surrounding your future home.
new_p_181: Locomotive Park is practically in your backyard, perfect for weekend family outings.
new_p_182: And Grand Canyon Caverns? Less than an hour away for when you want real adventure.
new_p_183: Walmart is a quick drive for all your essentials. Kingman Airport is nearby so your guests can find you with ease.
new_p_184: The Powerhouse Visitor Center and historic Bonelli House give you culture and entertainment whenever you want it.
new_p_187: Pay that today, and the land is legally yours.

new_p_192: No monthly bills. No waiting. Just instant, complete ownership of 0.21 acres with paved access, mountain views, and total freedom.

new_p_197: If it doesn't feel perfect, you get every penny back. No pressure. No questions asked.

new_p_220:
The
Arizona Land Deal That Lets You Build, Camp, And Live Exactly How You Want


Most land comes
with a mile-long list of rules. 


HOAs telling you
what color your curtains can be. Restrictions on what you can build. Fees for
breathing wrong. But not this land.




This is 0.21 acres
of pure freedom in Mohave County, Arizona. 


No HOA. No
busybodies. No one telling you how to live your life. Just you, your land, and
endless Arizona skies.




Here's
exactly what you're getting:


This land is zoned
RMH, which means Residential Manufactured Housing. 


Translation? You
can build your manufactured home right here. It's approved. It's legal. It's
ready for you to make it yours.




But here's where it
gets really good. You can camp on this land RIGHT NOW. Tent camping or RV
camping, up to 14 days at a time. 


That's 30 days
total per year. Which means you can use your land this weekend while you're
planning your build.




Bring your RV. Park
it. Spend weekends there. Get a feel for the land. Watch the sunsets.  Sleep under the stars. All while you're
figuring out your long-term plans.




Power options? 


You've got choices.
Hook up to the local electric company if you want traditional power. Or go
completely off-grid with solar or wind. Your land. Your rules.


Water works on a
haul system, just like most residents in the area. It's your off-grid water
solution. Simple. Proven. Reliable.




And the best part? 


Direct paved road
access. Your regular car gets you there. No 4x4 needed. No mud. No struggling
through dirt roads. Just smooth driving right to your property.




You own it with a
Warranty Deed. Real ownership. Real security. Legal protection that can't be
taken away.




What
makes this location special:


You're sitting on
historic Route 66. That's not just a road. That's a piece of American history.


A living, breathing
landmark that people travel from around the world to see. And you get to own a
piece of it.




The views? 


Stunning mountain
vistas from your property. The kind of scenery that makes you stop and stare
every single morning.




Kingman is close
by. Schools for the grandkids. Shops for groceries. 


Restaurants for
date night. Everything you need without being crowded or noisy.




The Arizona Route
66 Museum brings history to life just minutes away. 


Keepers of the Wild
Nature Park shows you the natural beauty surrounding your future home. 




Locomotive Park is
practically in your backyard, perfect for weekend family outings. 


And Grand Canyon
Caverns? Less than an hour away for when you want real adventure.




Walmart is a quick
drive for all your essentials. Kingman Airport is nearby so your guests can
find you with ease. 


The Powerhouse
Visitor Center and historic Bonelli House give you culture and entertainment
whenever you want it.

due_diligence_h2_1: Due Diligence, Presented as a Clear Checklist
due_diligence_h2_2: What to Check Before Moving Forward
due_diligence_h2_5: Want a Review with Inspection Tips?
due_diligence_p_25: Before purchasing land in the United States, it's essential to conduct comprehensive due diligence. We recommend verifying property access, zoning and land use, infrastructure availability (electricity, water, sewer), and flood or fire risks. Additionally, it's important to check local regulations, taxation, and relevant building permits.
due_diligence_p_26: Our goal is to provide you with all the information needed to make informed and secure decisions. Peleg Group accompanies you at every step, from property identification to closing the deal, ensuring you're equipped with all the necessary data for a successful investment in American land.
due_diligence_p_27: Use this page as a conversation guide when requesting a detailed property overview.
due_diligence_p_28: Verify legal access route and recorded rights of way
due_diligence_p_29: Check if there's an approved private or public road
due_diligence_p_30: Ensure there are no future restrictions on access rights
due_diligence_p_31: Ask the county about minimum distances and permitted uses
due_diligence_p_32: Check zoning regulations and future building restrictions
due_diligence_p_33: Ensure the designation matches your development plans
due_diligence_p_34: Determine availability of electricity, water, and sewer in the area
due_diligence_p_35: Estimate costs for connecting to public utilities
due_diligence_p_36: Check alternative options like private well or solar system
due_diligence_p_37: Survey the land to identify slopes and drainage areas
due_diligence_p_38: Check flood risks and extreme weather history
due_diligence_p_39: Ensure the soil is suitable for construction and doesn't require expensive work
due_diligence_p_40: Check history of property tax and assessment payments
due_diligence_p_41: Verify there are no liens or debts recorded on the property
due_diligence_p_42: Estimate annual tax costs for budget planning
due_diligence_p_43: Tour the area to identify factories, busy roads, or noise
due_diligence_p_44: Check future development plans that might affect the property
due_diligence_p_45: Talk to local residents to get a complete picture
due_diligence_p_46: Understand the building permit process in the county
due_diligence_p_47: Determine estimated waiting times and special requirements
due_diligence_p_48: Consult with a local contractor about known challenges
due_diligence_p_49: Assess resale potential and demand in the area
due_diligence_p_50: Check local market trends and recent transaction prices
due_diligence_p_51: Plan a clear exit strategy before making the purchase
due_diligence_p_52: A property that appears adjacent to a public road may be separated from it by a private right-of-way strip or reserved strip with no legal access. We checked historical ownership records and discovered that the nearby road is not recorded as a right of way for the property in question, and previous owners used an unrecorded neighbor agreement. Without legal updating, the buyer could find themselves without legal access.
due_diligence_p_53: Key lesson: Checking rights of way in county records is essential even when access appears straightforward.
due_diligence_p_54: A power line running 200 meters from the property doesn't guarantee cheap or even possible connection. We checked with the local electric company and discovered that extending the line would cost over $50,000, and the company requires advance guarantee. In other cases, the nearest water source was a neighbor's private well, with no shared usage rights. Early coordination with service providers reveals the true costs.
due_diligence_p_55: Key lesson: Physical proximity to services doesn't equal legal or economic availability—check with providers.
due_diligence_p_56: Zoning regulations in rural counties are sometimes vague or not updated on the website. We spoke directly with the planning department and they clarified that a certain agricultural area requires a minimum of 10 acres for residential construction, not 5 as written in an old document. Additionally, we discovered restrictions on certain building types that don't appear in online searches. A short phone call saves months of frustration.
due_diligence_p_57: Key lesson: Direct coordination with county authorities provides accurate answers and prevents legal surprises.
due_diligence_p_58: Check future zoning plans, building restrictions, and planned projects in the area. This information is crucial for understanding the property's long-term potential.
due_diligence_p_59: Get access to official maps, APN numbers, tax history, and property values. This information allows you to assess annual maintenance costs and ensure coordination with ownership records.
due_diligence_p_60: Verify legal access to the parcel, existing rights of way, and condition of adjacent roads. Parcels without proven access can be problematic for future sale or development.
due_diligence_p_61: Check FEMA maps for flood risks and federal fire risk ratings. Understanding natural risks is essential for insurance, obtaining credit, and future planning.
due_diligence_p_62: Intended use: Are you looking for land for residential, investment, agricultural, or commercial development?
due_diligence_p_63: Preferred area: Specific states or counties that interest you in the United States?
due_diligence_p_64: Access requirements: Do you need paved access, electricity or water connection, or are you interested in remote land?
due_diligence_p_65: We'll share with you a concise overview and the worthwhile questions to ask going forward for the parcels you're considering.
due_diligence_p_70: Peleg Group LLC - Leading in real estate with professional and customized solutions for every client.

            However, no method of transmission or storage is 100% secure.
legal_privacy_p_17: We may update this privacy policy from time to time. Material changes will be published on the website with 30 days advance notice.
legal_privacy_p_18: For questions or requests regarding this privacy policy, you can contact us:
legal_privacy_p_19: Database Registrar: For complaints, you can contact the Database Registrar at the Ministry of Justice

opportunities_h3_6: Tell Us What You're Looking For
opportunities_p_20: A selection of US land packages presented with consistent overviews—so you can quickly compare and request details when a parcel fits your strategy.
opportunities_p_25: Add your text content here. This is placeholder text that can be edited.
opportunities_p_29: Access appears via maintained county road; verify easement rights and utility connections.
opportunities_p_31: When you come to us with a preferred list or specific criteria, we don't just send links – we prepare a professional overview for you. You'll receive a detailed summary of each parcel, precise location context, and a list of warning signs worth checking with local authorities or agents. This isn't a substitute for full due diligence, but it gives you the tools to decide whether to move forward or move to the next option.
opportunities_p_32: Our process is built to save you time and provide clarity. Instead of navigating alone through public documents and complex maps, you receive an organized and structured information package – with all the relevant details needed to ask the right questions and move forward with confidence.
opportunities_p_33: Share your criteria with us – parcel size, preferred area, budget, or any special requirements. This can be a brief conversation or detailed form, depending on what's comfortable for you.
opportunities_p_34: We'll send you an organized document with parcel details, precise location, satellite images, and basic area data. Everything in one place, ready for review.
opportunities_p_35: We'll highlight critical points for verification – access rights, zoning restrictions, available infrastructure, and more. These are the questions worth asking the county or agent before making a final decision.
opportunities_p_36: With all the information in hand, you can decide with confidence – proceed to full due diligence, request additional details, or move to another option. You're in complete control.
opportunities_p_37: Detailed parcel overview with all basic data
opportunities_p_38: Complete location context – maps, access, proximity to infrastructure and services
opportunities_p_39: Risk list and verification points with authorities and agents
opportunities_p_40: Suggested questions to ask before making a final decision
opportunities_p_41: We analyze market trends, demand data, development potential, and accessibility. Each parcel undergoes comprehensive due diligence to ensure it meets high investment standards and offers long-term growth potential for our investors.
opportunities_p_42: Yes, we provide detailed analysis of comparable sales in the area, market trends, and current valuation data. Our information package includes maps, legal documentation, and financial data to help you make informed decisions.
opportunities_p_43: It's recommended to verify property rights, zoning restrictions, access rights, water rights, and environmental history. We provide basic documents, but it's advisable to consult with a local attorney or real estate expert for complete verification.
opportunities_p_44: After receiving all information and completing due diligence, most transactions can close within 2-4 weeks. The process depends on your financing approval speed, document review, and closing times with the title company.
opportunities_p_45: We specialize in growth areas throughout the United States, including Texas, Arizona, Florida, and southeastern states. We identify markets with strong infrastructure development, population growth, and positive economic conditions.
opportunities_p_46: Contact us through our contact page or email us directly. We'll provide you with a detailed information package including maps, legal documents, financial analysis, and parcel photos.
opportunities_p_47: We'll respond with a clear summary and next due diligence steps—no noise, no pressure.
